races.name: Itzulia Basque Country – Stage 4
races.description: Stage 4 of Itzulia Basque Country on a 167.2km circuit around Galdakao featuring seven categorised climbs and 3,000m+ of elevation. The stage is roller-coaster in nature, with flatter sections between climbs giving the GC riders rest before the decisive final sequence. A Red Bull KM bonus sprint (6-4-2 seconds) features before the final climb. The decisive finale: Alto de El Vivero (4.3km @ 8.3%), cresting 29km from the line, followed by a penultimate climb (~3.2km @ 8-10% first half), summit 9km out, a technical descent back into Galdakao, then an uphill finish. With del Toro and Landa already gone, and Seixas dominant, the stage is likely a battle for the breakaway or a GC consolidation day — but the difficulty is high enough for Roglič or others to probe.

gc_standings_before_stage.source: After Stage 3, Basauri – confirmed from official Itzulia site
abandonments: Isaac del Toro (UAE) – crashed Stage 3, muscle tear, out
Mikel Landa (Soudal) – crashed Stage 2 descent, out (Giro d'Italia in doubt)

stage_features: Red Bull KM bonus sprint (~13km from finish): 6s / 4s / 2s time bonuses
races.rider_notes: Seixas leads by 1:59 with two Red Bull riders (Roglič & Lipowitz) as closest challengers. Red Bull have numerical advantage for GC attacks. Seixas has signalled a more conservative approach after winning the first two stages, though he didn't completely rule out another aggressive day. Roglič has shown he is still competitive despite losing his Itzulia ITT record. Skjelmose climbed up to 4th in stage 2 and is a consistent finisher. Ben Tulett (Visma-Lease a Bike) has been quietly consistent at +2:27. Isaac del Toro's abandon leaves UAE without a GC card. Juan Ayuso continues to struggle badly and is effectively out of GC contention.

broadcasts.notes: Free-to-air on EITB Basque public broadcaster from race start.
Live coverage from 14:30 BST (13:30 UTC) daily. Plans from £25.99/month.
From 9:30 AM EDT (13:30 UTC). Monthly $39.99 / annual $155.88.
Eurosport and HBO Max / Discovery+ across Europe from ~14:30 CEST.

data_confidence.notes: Stage 4 route Galdakao–Galdakao confirmed by official Itzulia site and all preview sources. 167.2km and 3,000m+ confirmed (Domestique: '3,000 metres of elevation gain, across 167.2km around Galdakao'). Key climbs: Alto de El Vivero 4.3km @ 8.3%, summit 29km from finish; penultimate climb ~3.2km @ 8-10%, summit 9km from finish; uphill finish at Galdakao. Red Bull KM bonus sprint included. Start 12:20 CEST / finish ~16:30 CEST confirmed by CyclingUpToDate stage 4 preview. GC standings after Stage 3 confirmed from official Itzulia website results page: Seixas 8:08:24, Roglič +1:59, Lipowitz +2:08, Skjelmose +2:14, Tulett +2:27.

races.name: Région Pays de la Loire Tour – Stage 3
races.stage_race_name: Région Pays de la Loire Tour 2026
races.description: Stage 3 of the Région Pays de la Loire Tour — the queen stage of this ProSeries race. At 199km with ~2,000m of elevation gain and a final circuit around Sainte-Suzanne-et-Chammes (one of France's 'Plus Belles Petites Cités de Caractère'), it is the most demanding stage of the four-day race. The final circuit features the Côte de la Fousillère (800m @ 6.4%) and finishes on a false-flat uphill stretch (~7.5% in the final hectometres) that firmly favours the GC leaders over pure sprinters. This is the decisive day before the Le Mans finale on Friday. Ethan Vernon (NSN) has won both Stages 1 and 2 in bunch sprints — but today's uphill finish should end his winning streak and decide who takes the overall lead.

gc_standings_before_stage.stage_2_winner: Ethan Vernon (NSN Cycling Team) – 2nd consecutive sprint win
gc_standings_before_stage.note: Vernon leads on time after two sprint finishes. Full GC gaps not confirmed from search — stage has been leader by stage time.

races.rider_notes: Today's uphill finish should see the puncheurs come to the fore and the GC settle properly. Gautherat (Decathlon CMA CGM) won Paris-Camembert and Adélie de Vitré earlier this spring — perfectly suited to this profile. Costiou (Groupama-FDJ United) was rated top favourite pre-race. Hermans (Pinarello-Q36.5) is a consistent puncheur who excels on short, punchy climbs.

broadcasts.notes: All stages broadcast live on L'Équipe 21 (free-to-air in France). Broadcast window covers the final ~2hrs — likely from ~15:30 CEST given a ~17:30 finish. Exact start time not confirmed.

data_confidence.notes: Stage 3 route confirmed: Avrillé → Sainte-Suzanne-et-Chammes, 199km (per Cyclismactu, TotalVelo, DirectVelo). Queen stage with uphill finish confirmed. Elevation ~2,000m per TotalVelo (who says 'dénivelé positif approchant les 2,000 mètres'). Start time estimated ~11:30 CEST — not officially confirmed. Stage 2 winner confirmed as Ethan Vernon (Cyclingnews: 'Pays de la Loire Tour stage 2: Ethan Vernon snaps up a second straight bunch sprint win'). L'Équipe 21 broadcast confirmed. No international broadcaster identified.

recent_results.notes: Merlier's 3rd consecutive Scheldeprijs (2024, 2025, 2026). Returned from lengthy knee injury (his 2nd race of 2026). Jasper Philipsen boxed in and unable to respond. Dylan Groenewegen crashed in final 10km. Merlier launched from 250m, beat his chest in emotional celebration.

Kool finally broke her run of three consecutive runner-up finishes at Scheldeprijs. Race was crash-marred — heavy crash inside final 2km reduced the group. Kool was perfectly positioned, launched 150m from line. Lorena Wiebes not racing (4-time winner).

recent_results.race: Région Pays de la Loire Tour Stage 2 (Pro) – La Garnache → Les Sables-d'Olonne
recent_results.notes: Vernon's second consecutive sprint win at the race (also won Stage 1). 4 wins total for Vernon in 2026.

recent_results.race: Itzulia Basque Country Stage 3 (WT) – Basauri circuit, 152.8km
recent_results.notes: Laurance and Arrieta split from a 16-rider break at the top of Sarasola with ~25km to go. Laurance launched off Arrieta's wheel in the uphill sprint. GC unchanged: Seixas +1:59 over Roglič. Isaac del Toro crashed and abandoned Stage 3 with a muscle tear. The driver of the race doctor's car was expelled from the race after being found to have caused Mikel Landa's Stage 2 crash.

notes: Paul Seixas leads Itzulia by 1:59 after three stages — won Stages 1 (ITT) and 2 (mountain solo), controlled Stage 3. The 19-year-old from Decathlon is in extraordinary form and this is becoming a historic run.
Isaac del Toro abandoned Itzulia with a muscle tear from a Stage 3 crash — his Ardennes plans are now in doubt.
Mikel Landa also abandoned after his Stage 2 crash; his entire 2026 season including Giro d'Italia preparation is disrupted. The driver of the race doctor's car was formally expelled.
Tim Merlier returned from a lengthy knee injury to win Scheldeprijs for the third consecutive year — only his second race of 2026.
Charlotte Kool ended a frustrating run of three second-place finishes at Scheldeprijs with a commanding win.

Paris-Roubaix is this Sunday (April 12). Pogačar targets his 5th Monument for a Grand Slam that only Merckx, Van Looy, and De Vlaeminck have achieved. Evenepoel will not start. Reusser and Le Court-Pienaar out of Roubaix Femmes.

upcoming.notes: Queen stage. 176.2km, 3,841m. 8 categorised climbs including Cat.1 Krabelin (5km @ 9.6%, 67km to go) and Cat.1 Izua (4.1km @ 9.25%, 27km to go). Decisive GC day.

upcoming.race: Région Pays de la Loire Tour Stage 4 (Pro) – Brûlon → Le Mans
upcoming.notes: Final stage, 179.5km. Le Mans city circuit. Côte de Gazonfier (short, ~15% max) and Côte de Saint-Blaise in the finishing circuit. Decides overall winner.
258.3km, Compiègne → Roubaix. Pogačar chases the Grand Slam — 5th Monument. Philipsen and Ganna among top sprint contenders.
143.1km, Denain → Roubaix. Without Reusser (fractured bones from Tour of Flanders) and Le Court-Pienaar.

issues: Itzulia Stage 4 'penultimate climb' described as 3.2km @ 8% by CyclingUpToDate vs 3.2km @ 7.7% avg by Cyclist. The 7.7% is confirmed as the 'Legina' climb — first half averages 10%, overall avg ~8%. Used 8% avg with 10% max for accuracy.

Région Pays de la Loire Tour Stage 3 start time not officially confirmed — estimated ~11:30 CEST based on 199km at ~38kph avg reaching ~17:30 finish. This is a longer stage than previous days so may start earlier.
Région Pays de la Loire Tour Stage 3 GC standings after Stage 2 not fully retrieved — Vernon confirmed as stage winner and leads on GC by time bonuses, but full seconds gaps not available.
